background.problem_statement: The building management needs to identify unusual energy consumption patterns to optimize costs, detect equipment malfunctions, and ensure efficient operations.
background.business_context: Energy costs represent a significant operational expense. Detecting anomalies early can prevent waste, identify security issues (e.g., equipment left on), and optimize HVAC schedules.

floor_details.description: Ground floor with standard office spaces
Second floor with computer labs and workstations
Third floor with meeting rooms and offices

categories.name: air_con
categories.description: HVAC system consumption including cooling and ventilation
categories.typical_pattern: High during office hours (9am-7pm), low during nights and weekends

categories.name: computer_appliance
categories.description: Computers, servers, printers, and other electronic equipment
categories.typical_pattern: High during office hours, minimal during nights, potential after-hours usage

categories.name: lobby_corridor_lighting
categories.typical_pattern: Moderate and consistent throughout the day, slightly higher during office hours
